items.front: Minimum distance outside approach gate for vectors to final (5-9-1a)
items.back: At least 2 miles outside the approach gate, unless ceiling ≥500 ft above MVA/MIA and visibility ≥3 miles (then closer but no closer than the approach gate), or pilot specifically requests (then inside approach gate but no closer than FAF).

items.front: Separation between aircraft on opposite base legs (5-9-1b)
items.back: Minimum of 1,000 feet vertical separation between aircraft on opposite base legs unless another form of approved separation is established during turn-on to final.
Not above the glideslope/glidepath or below the minimum glideslope intercept altitude specified on the approach procedure chart.
At an altitude which will allow descent in accordance with the published procedure.

items.front: En route vector to final - radar scope requirements (5-9-1e)
items.back: Approach gate and final approach course line must be displayed. Precision: line extends at least max localizer range. Nonprecision: line extends at least 10 NM outside approach gate. Max range 150 NM, or adjacent display at ≤125 NM configured for the approach. Otherwise use 4-8-1.
<2 miles from approach gate or triple simultaneous: 20° max. ≥2 miles: 30° max (45° for helicopters).

items.front: Deviations after course interception - outside approach gate (5-9-2b1)
items.back: Apply interception angle procedures; if necessary, vector for another approach.

items.front: Deviations after course interception - inside approach gate (5-9-2b2)
items.back: Inform the pilot of the aircraft's position and ask intentions. Phraseology: '(Ident) (distance) MILE(S) FROM THE AIRPORT, (distance) MILE(S) RIGHT/LEFT OF COURSE, SAY INTENTIONS.'

items.front: En route - radar range above 125 NM for final approach (5-9-2c)
items.back: Controller must solicit and receive a pilot report that the aircraft is established on the final approach course. If pilot has not reported established by the FAF, inform pilot of observed position and ask intentions.

Inform the aircraft whenever a vector will take it across the final approach course and state the reason. Phraseology: 'EXPECT VECTORS ACROSS FINAL FOR (purpose).' Pilot is not expected to turn inbound unless approach clearance has been issued.
Issue: (a) position relative to a fix on the final approach course (or to the navaid/airport if none); (b) vector to intercept final if required; (c) approach clearance (except radar approaches).

items.front: When approach clearance may be issued (5-9-4c)
items.back: Only after the aircraft is: (1) established on a segment of a published route or IAP, or (2) assigned an altitude to maintain until established on a segment of a published route or IAP.

items.front: Simultaneous independent parallel approaches - separation during turn-on (5-9-5)
items.back: Minimum 1,000 feet vertical or 3 miles radar separation between aircraft during turn-on to parallel final. Provide minimum applicable radar separation between aircraft on the same final approach course.
Minimum of 1.5 miles radar separation between aircraft on adjacent final approach courses. Also applies to EoR (Established on RNP) operations.

items.front: Simultaneous independent close parallel approaches - runway spacing (5-9-7)
items.back: Runway centerlines separated by at least 4,300 ft but no more than 9,000 ft (field elev ≤5,000 ft MSL), or at least 5,000 ft but no more than 9,200 ft (field elev >5,000 ft MSL).

NTZ established equal distance between extended runway final approach courses. Primary responsibility for navigation on final rests with the PILOT. Control instructions only to ensure separation and prevent NTZ penetration.

For PRM: provide position info if aircraft may penetrate NTZ. Instruct return to course if overshoot/penetration. If NTZ penetration, instruct adjacent aircraft to alter course (may include climb/descent - descent not below MVA). Terminate monitoring: visual separation applied, aircraft reports approach lights/runway in sight, or ≤1 mi from threshold.
Communications transfer to tower controller's frequency must be completed prior to losing 1,000 ft vertical or 3 miles radar separation between aircraft.
PRM approaches MUST be assigned when conducting instrument approaches to dual and triple parallel runways with runway centerlines separated by less than 4,300 feet.
Parallel runways with centerlines separated by at least 750 ft and less than 3,000 ft, with one final approach course offset by 2.5 to 3.0 degrees. Requires FAA authorization.

Minimum 1,000 ft vertical or 3 miles radar during turn-on. Minimum applicable radar separation on same final. If centerlines <2,500 ft apart: also minimum radar separation between trailing offset of leading pair and lead straight-in of subsequent pair.

items.front: SOIA - wake turbulence for runways <2,500 ft apart (5-9-9g)
items.back: Ceiling ≥500 ft above MVA: wake vortex spacing not required. Ceiling <500 ft above MVA: wake vortex spacing per 5-5-4 must be applied unless mitigating techniques authorized by AJT-1. Issue all applicable wake turbulence advisories.

Trailing offset pilot: after offset approach MAP, assumes visual separation from straight-in traffic. If visual contact lost, advise ATC and fly published missed approach. Must have straight-in traffic and runway environment in sight to continue past offset MAP.
Runway centerlines separated by >9,000 ft (field elev ≤5,000 ft MSL) or >9,200 ft (field elev >5,000 ft MSL). No final monitors required. No NTZ requirement.

If aircraft overshoots turn-on or deviates toward adjacent approach: instruct to return to correct final. If deviation threatens adjacent: TRAFFIC ALERT, turn immediately, climb and maintain altitude. Consider weather factors (wind, wind shear, severe weather).

items.front: Transitional procedure - go-around during reduced separation (5-9-11)
items.back: When one aircraft go-arounds or has clearance terminated during simultaneous dependent/independent/reduced-separation approaches: expeditiously issue control instructions to increase separation. Establish approved separation (altitude and/or lateral via divergence). Issue wake turbulence cautionary advisories per 2-1-20.
